title: Developer vows to present more ‘options’ for Davis Square tower project amid neighborhood opposition
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Developer Copper Mill plans to present more options for a proposed 26-story tower in Somerville's Davis Square.
The development includes 502 units, with 126 affordable housing apartments and a focus on minimizing parking.
Community members express concerns about the project size and neighborhood fit, leading to promises of more engagement.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of his twin nephews, Sebastian and Zachary, with Down syndrome.
The Massachusetts Down Syndrome Congress (MDSC) supported Ponte's family after his nephews' diagnoses and hospital stays.
Ponte's run aims to honor the memory of Sebastian and support individuals with Down syndrome like Zachary.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston firefighter and National Guard Major Mark Garcia was injured in an Iranian drone strike in Kuwait.
The drone strike killed six U.S. soldiers; Garcia is currently recovering in Germany.
Garcia serves with Boston's Engine 50 and is praised by the Firefighters IAFF Local 718 union for his dedication.

title: Lowell murder trial delayed after lead State Police investigator allegedly causes fatal crash while driving drunk
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lowell murder retrial delayed indefinitely due to investigator's involvement in a fatal drunk driving crash.
State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley investigated the 2020 Lowell murder; he allegedly caused a 2023 crash.
Justice Barry-Smith cites ongoing Quigley investigation as a reason for trial delays without a new date.

title: ​Bruins’ playoff hopes might rest on Jeremy Swayman’s shoulders for this stretch run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman has a .906 save percentage and ranks third in the NHL with 21.4 goals saved above expected.
The Bruins are currently in the second Wild Card spot with a 36-22-6 record, just one point behind the Red Wings.
Boston's coach Marco Sturm plans to rely more on Swayman as they play 11 of the last 18 games away from TD Garden.

title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Body cam footage shows police arriving at Estella in Boston during an unauthorized party involving Patriots players on Jan. 27.
Officers found the party included hookahs, liquor bottles, and unauthorized adult entertainment. No players were positively identified on video.
Estella's owner received a three-day license suspension after the incident; the board deferred two days for a year.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots signed safety Kevin Byard to a one-year, $9 million deal after an All-Pro season with the Bears.
Byard, 33, is a durable player with 36 interceptions over 10 NFL seasons, never missing a game due to injury.
Byard previously played under Patriots coach Mike Vrabel and earned three First Team All-Pro honors in his career.

title: Man resentenced in 2011 slaying of Dorchester teen
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Nyasani Watt was resentenced to 14-17 years for the 2011 shooting of a Dorchester teen, eligible for parole immediately.
The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court reversed Watt's original murder conviction due to denial of right to counsel in 2024.
Victim Jaivon Blake, 16, was fatally shot while walking home; co-defendant Sheldon Mattis also received life with parole possibility.

title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams launches 'Our City. Our Beer.' variety pack with beers inspired by Boston sports stars Derrick White, Garrett Crochet, Julian Edelman, and Zdeno Chara.
The variety pack includes four beers: White Ale, Crochet's Pale Ale, Julian Edel-Brau Lager, and Big Z Pilsner.
A public launch event is planned for April 4 at Banners Kitchen & Tap in Boston, with appearances by the featured athletes.

title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Dave McGillivray will run his 54th Boston Marathon with his children and retired astronaut Sunita Williams.
McGillivray has run every Boston Marathon since 1973 after a failed attempt as a high school senior in 1972.
Max and Elle McGillivray are fundraising for Justice Health Initiative and Finish Strong Foundation respectively.
